repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Transcripts of confessions obtained from prisoners at Tuol Sleng Prison, Phnom Penh, Cambodia. Also, five thousand photographs of Tuol Sleng prisoners.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Transcripts of confessions obtained from prisoners at Tuol Sleng Prison, Phnom Penh, Cambodia. Also, five thousand photographs of Tuol Sleng prisoners.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIAL
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Guide to Cambodia Documentation Commission Records (collection #4499)

See also: Yale University Cambodian Genocide Program Database for more detailed information about what is on our microfilm. To use this database for materials from this collection, search under "Sources" for "Tuol Sleng Catalogue of Confessions".

p.emph: The references are listed in alphabetical order by detainees' given names. Each reference includes a microfilm reel number and item number (frame) indicating the order by which the confessions appear on the reels. If available, the age, gender, entry dates (in the date column), confession dates, and nationality are also listed.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Tuol Sleng Prison Confessions and Photographs, #4883. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

acqinfo.head: PROVENANCE
acqinfo.p: The records in this collection were filmed under the direction of the Cornell University Library's John M. Echols Collection on Southeast Asia and the Department of Conservation and Preservation at the Tuol Sleng museum of Genocide in Phnom Penh, Cambodia, from January 1991 to April 1993. The confessions of some 4300 detainees comprising 400,000 pages of handwritten text in Khmer, as well as internal prison documents were filmed. This project was part of a larger effort to preserve and film Cambodian manuscripts at five locations in Phnom Penh.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The records in this collection were filmed under the direction of the Cornell University Library's John M. Echols Collection on Southeast Asia and the Department of Conservation and Preservation at the Tuol Sleng Museum of Genocide in Phnom Penh, Cambodia, from January 1991 to April 1993. The confessions of some 4300 detainees comprising 400,000 pages of handwritten text in Khmer, as well as internal prison documents were filmed. This project was part of a larger effort to preserve and film Cambodian manuscripts at five locations in Phnom Penh.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: For personal research only. Not for commercial use.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: Reproductions may not be made. Originals are owned by the Tuol Sleng Museum of Genocide in Phnom Penh, Cambodia.
